message:
feat(frankenpandas): expose remaining fp-runtime types (fd90.265 / br-aqre9)

Eight fp-runtime types + 1 feature-gated function were unreachable
from frankenpandas:

- LossMatrix — README line 403 explicitly references "asymmetric
  loss matrix penalizes 'allow if incompatible' (100.0)"
- ConformalGuard, ConformalPredictionSet — conformal prediction
- DecodeProof, RaptorQEnvelope, RaptorQMetadata — RaptorQ
  erasure-coding artifact surface
- ScrubStatus — repair status
- RuntimeError — runtime error type
- outcome_to_action (#[cfg(feature = "asupersync")]) — helper that
  bridges asupersync::Outcome → DecisionAction

Feature-gating gotcha: outcome_to_action is gated behind asupersync
in fp-runtime, so the re-export is wrapped in
#[cfg(feature = "asupersync")] in frankenpandas as well.

All 9 kept out of prelude — advanced surface that most users won't touch.

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-aqre9","title":"fd90.265: Expose remaining fp-runtime types (LossMatrix/ConformalPredictionSet/etc.)","description":"fd90.265: Expose remaining fp-runtime items (LossMatrix/ConformalPredictionSet/etc.).

Several fp-runtime types are unreachable from frankenpandas:
- LossMatrix — README line 403 explicitly references "asymmetric loss matrix"
- ConformalGuard, ConformalPredictionSet — conformal prediction surface
- DecodeProof, RaptorQEnvelope, RaptorQMetadata — RaptorQ erasure-coding artifact surface
- ScrubStatus — repair status enum
- RuntimeError — runtime error type
- outcome_to_action — helper

Scope:
- Add 9 items to top-level fp_runtime re-export
- Skip prelude (these are advanced — most users won't touch them)

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.
Time estimate: 10-15min.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T08:12:20.092089034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T08:14:32.903597806Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T08:14:32.901577950Z","close_reason":"Exposed 8 fp-runtime types at top-level + 1 feature-gated function: LossMatrix (README line 403 references the asymmetric loss matrix), ConformalGuard, ConformalPredictionSet, DecodeProof, RaptorQEnvelope, RaptorQMetadata, RuntimeError, ScrubStatus, plus #[cfg(feature='asupersync')] outcome_to_action helper. All advanced — kept out of prelude. Caught feature-gating: outcome_to_action is gated behind the asupersync feature, so wrapped its re-export in #[cfg(feature='asupersync')]. All 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-arnn","title":"[LOW] fp-conformance assert_excel_roundtrip helper diverges from public default to satisfy reader; document as test-only gap","description":"Reviewing 989b/mxho fixes (cc): both excel round-trip helpers were patched to write with index=false because the default reader doesn't promote the leading index column back. Public write_excel_bytes default remains pandas-aligned (index=true). This is a test-only divergence — the harness now exercises a non-default code path.

Risk: production users calling write_excel_bytes(default) and read_excel_bytes(default) will hit the same column-leak bug the harness sidesteps. The conformance suite no longer surfaces this regression because the harness avoids it.

Suggested follow-up:
1. Add a separate fixture (or augment FP-P2D-129/excel_round_trip_basic_strict) that writes with index=true and reads with the matching index_col so the default-pair round-trip is genuinely tested end-to-end.
2. Or: ship a read_excel_bytes default that auto-detects the leading index column emitted by write_excel_bytes (via a sentinel header or 'inferred index_col' option) so the defaults round-trip cleanly.

Low priority: doesn't block conformance-to-zero work, but the test harness is currently 'green for the wrong reason' on this surface.","notes":"cc-pandas triage 2026-04-22: the inline comment at crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:5020-5025 already documents the helper's divergence (index=false) from the public write_excel_bytes default (index=true), citing br-989b. Both suggested follow-ups (fixture with matching index_col, or reader-side index auto-detection) are product-level scope decisions rather than conformance-gate bugs and do not block conformance-to-zero. Deferring the follow-up as a documented known issue; reopen if the default write/read pair is reprioritized.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:05:04.811757056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:44:49.723965057Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:44:49.721417028Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
